timeframe_stack._description: Three-timeframe architecture. Each layer has a distinct job. Never collapse all three into one chart — they answer different questions.

daily_weekly.question: Which direction are we trading this week?
what_to_read: Weekly chart: EMA200 slope — macro regime, bull or bear
Weekly chart: prior week high/low — key levels NY will respect
Daily chart: EMA20/50/200 stack — is trend intact or breaking down?
Daily chart: unfilled daily FVGs — strongest price magnets on the map
Daily chart: swing highs and lows from last 20 sessions
Daily close relative to VWAP — institutional positioning heading into next session
Weekly bias set Sunday by market-environment-strategist agent — LONG / SHORT / NEUTRAL
daily_weekly.output: Directional bias for the week. LONG, SHORT, or NO BIAS. This is the filter — only take 4H setups that align with daily/weekly bias. Countertrend setups require 9+/10 score minimum.
daily_weekly.cadence: Set Sunday. Update only if daily closes a candle that breaks the macro structure — not intraday.

four_hour.question: Where are the high-probability entry zones forming?
what_to_read: EMA20/50/200 stack on 4H — is the intermediate trend confirming the daily bias?
Unfilled 4H FVGs — map all active gaps, note age and size
4H EMA20 as dynamic support/resistance — where is price likely to pull back to?
RSI on 4H — is momentum resetting into the setup zone (35-55 longs, 45-65 shorts)?
MACD histogram on 4H — is it turning in trade direction?
VWAP on 4H — is institutional flow aligned with bias?
HTF level proximity — is there a daily/weekly level within 0.50 that caps the trade?
four_hour.output: A defined setup zone — a specific price range where an entry trigger is valid. Not the entry itself. The zone. Example: 'Bullish setup zone: 78.20–78.45 (4H FVG + EMA20 confluence). Bias: LONG. Waiting for 15min trigger inside this zone.'
four_hour.cadence: Check at every 4H close during London prep window (8:00–9:30 AM ET). Update setup zone map before NY open.

fifteen_min.question: Is price showing a confirmed entry signal inside the 4H setup zone?
what_to_read: Price action inside the 4H setup zone — is price respecting or breaking the zone?
15min candle close inside the 4H FVG or at EMA20 — confirmation of zone hold
15min EMA structure — short-term EMAs turning in trade direction
15min RSI — momentum confirmation at the micro level (not oversold, showing turn)
15min VWAP — is price reclaiming or rejecting VWAP on the entry candle?
Volume on the 15min trigger candle — above-average volume = institutional participation
Candle pattern: pin bar, engulfing, or inside bar breakout inside the setup zone
fifteen_min.output: Entry price, stop placement (below/above the 15min trigger candle's structural low/high), and R/R confirmation. The 15min trigger IS the entry signal. No 15min confirmation = no trade, even if the 4H setup zone looks perfect.
fifteen_min.stop_guidance: Place stop below the low of the 15min trigger candle for longs, above the high for shorts. This is tighter than a 4H-derived stop — better R/R, but requires the 15min level to hold. If it doesn't, the setup failed at the trigger, not the thesis.
fifteen_min.cadence: Monitor live during NY open execution window (9:30 AM–12:00 PM ET). Alert fires when price enters a mapped 4H setup zone — then watch 15min for trigger.

strategy.name: CRUDE INTENTIONS — Three-Timeframe EMA + FVG Confluence System
strategy.description: Three-timeframe confluence system. Daily/Weekly defines macro bias and direction. 4H identifies setup zones — FVG maps, EMA confluence levels, RSI reset zones. 15min provides the entry trigger — candle confirmation inside the 4H zone with stop placed at the 15min structural level. All three layers must align. A+ confluence score minimum 7/10 before any entry fires. When in doubt — NO TRADE.

aplus_checklist.description: Score 1 point for each. Minimum 7/10 to take the trade. 10/10 = full size. 7-8/10 = standard size. Below 7 = NO TRADE.

checks: Daily EMA stack aligned — EMA20/50/200 all pointing in trade direction on the daily chart
Weekly bias confirms — Sunday output is LONG (for longs) or SHORT (for shorts), not NEUTRAL
4H EMA stack aligned — EMA20/50/200 intermediate trend confirms daily bias
Price inside a mapped 4H setup zone — at EMA20, inside an unfilled FVG, or at VWAP confluence
4H RSI in reset zone — 35-55 for longs, 45-65 for shorts
4H MACD histogram turning in trade direction
HTF structure clear — no major daily or weekly S/R within 0.50 capping the trade
Risk/reward valid — minimum 2:1 from 15min entry to first target
15min trigger candle confirmed — closed inside 4H setup zone showing rejection or reclaim
15min VWAP and session aligned — price reclaiming VWAP on trigger candle AND session is NY open

scoring.7_10: B+ — MEDIUM — standard to half size

aplus_checklist.eia_hard_block: EIA window (Wednesday 7:30 AM–1:30 PM ET) overrides the checklist. 10/10 during EIA = NO TRADE.

notes: v1.8 — Three-timeframe architecture formalized. Daily/Weekly = macro bias. 4H = setup identification. 15min = entry trigger. A+ checklist restructured into 5 layers. Entry stops now at 15min trigger candle structural level.
